Why do you want to work at Ramp?: A great people event isn't just a party on the calendar—from my experience, it's a designed experience that reflects a company's culture and gets measurably better each time through feedback and tighter systems. What pulls me to Ramp specifically is the high-agency, builder culture: the chance to own internal and recruiting events from the ground up out of the NYC HQ, rather than inherit someone else's playbook. At InGenius Prep I own end-to-end planning for 20+ programs and live events and build the scalable templates that keep a busy calendar consistent. I genuinely love the operational craft of making a large event feel effortless to everyone in the room — and I want to do that at a place that treats it as real work.
Tell us about an event or program you bu: At Kronos Research I owned recruiting events end-to-end — moderating C-suite panels at top universities that reached 230+ students. The point wasn't the panel itself; it was whether it actually moved candidates, so I tied it to the funnel. Through personalized candidate engagement around those events, applications jumped 566% in two months. That taught me the difference between an event that feels good in the room and one that landed: you design backward from the outcome, then check the data afterward. I bring that same diagnose-then-measure habit to internal culture work — at Vestas I helped run well-being initiatives recognized among 1,000+ competitors, and I wanted to know they were working, not just that people showed up.
How do you think about building culture?: Culture programs are most durable when they travel and stick without the organizer in the room. At Vestas Wind Systems I strengthened culture through an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, and helped pilot a leadership program that scaled to 23 global locations — which only worked because we built it to be repeatable, not heroic. My approach is grounded in my MS in Applied Organizational Psychology (USC): I diagnose what people actually need, collaborate cross-functionally on the design, and track whether it's working. As an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, building culture that scales is the part of this work I find most energizing.
What makes you a fit for a Senior role h: I'll be candid: my title hasn't said 'Senior People Events' — but the work the role describes is the work I already do. At InGenius Prep I run end-to-end logistics, vendors, scheduling, and run-of-show for 20+ programs and events for 600+ participants, and I build the systems that let a busy programming calendar stay consistent and efficient. I'm comfortable owning ambiguity, partnering with Marketing and recruiting, and being the person who makes complexity feel effortless to everyone else. Ramp's pace and ownership culture are exactly the high-visibility challenge I want next.
